title: euphorbia nicaeensis subsp. demnatensis
intro: Euphorbia nicaeensis subsp. demnatensis, also known as the Atlas spurge, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its succulent stems and white or pale yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The Atlas spurge has been found to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating pain, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Euphorbia nicaeensis subsp. demnatensis can be made from the plant's roots, stems, or leaves, and are often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical ointments.
